creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
Martin, good day.

Sat, Dec 06, 2008 at 10:00:35PM +0000, miwi@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: [vuxml] databases/mantis: document vulnerabilities in 1.1.3 and 1.1.2
> 
> ports/129438
> State-Changed-When: Sat Dec 6 22:00:35 UTC 2008
> State-Changed-Why: 
> New port added, with minor changes. Thanks!

It's not a new port, but rather a new VuXML entry for CVE-2008-4687.
CVE-2008-4688 and CVE-2008-4689 are still not added -- is there a reason
for this?

Sat, Dec 06, 2008 at 10:41:47PM +0000, miwi@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> Synopsis: [patch] databases/mantis: upgrade to 1.1.5
> 
> ports/129437
> State-Changed-When: Sat Dec 6 22:41:47 UTC 2008
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Committed. Thanks!

Thanks!  But I have a comment ;))

Commit log for the port talks about
  http://www.vuxml.org/freebsd/29255141-c3df-11dd-a721-0030843d3802.html
  http://www.vuxml.org/freebsd/00d6040a-b8e0-11dd-a578-0030843d3802.html
They both aren't relevant to the upgrade path from 1.1.4 to 1.1.5 because
these entries are talking about old vulnerabilities in Mantis < 1.1.3.

And a side note:
http://www.vuxml.org/freebsd/00d6040a-b8e0-11dd-a578-0030843d3802.html
isn't the same as CVE-2008-4689, they are just both talking about
session cookies ;))
-- 
Eygene

 )/' _/     \   `-_,   /            #  while single-stepping the kernel.
